notes: Smallest possible round-trip: one user prompt, one assistant reply, no tool use, asserting the ready→working→ready arc with TotalTokens > 0 after the turn. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver, not headless `codex exec`: a single-shot `exec` turn is too short for the daemon to observe the ready→working flip (debounce coalesces it; the process exits first), and its turn-end surfaces as process_exited rather than a working→ready settle. The interactive REPL stays alive across the one turn, so the daemon observes the full working→ready arc on the live rollout session, then process_exited only at REPL teardown — the same shape that lets the sibling full-lifecycle-toolcall and multi-turn-conversation cells pass 100%, here for a single turn. The daemon sees proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then one working/ready flip. first_turn_end anchors to first_turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the earlier birth ready. The TotalTokens > 0 assertion the scenario validates isn't expressible in the phase DSL today (no metric form in the invariant DSL); it's covered by parser unit tests TestParser_TurnCompleteEmitsTurnDone / TestParser_MultiTurnTokenCount_UsesPerTurnSnapshot.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
User prompt → working on the rollout session
Assistant reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
